vibe_description: Objective, rigid, and functional. A modern, high-contrast B2B tool for contractors. Feels sturdy, precise, and highly readable in bright sunlight on a jobsite. Trades the dated forest green for a high-visibility 'Safety Orange' against stark white and concrete grays.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous container padding to separate sections, but extremely tight, tabular internal padding for data entry (Grid Borders pattern) to minimize scrolling during estimation.
spacing_and_layout.container: w-full max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8

surfaces_and_micro_themes.dashboard_cards: bg-white border border-[#E4E4E7] rounded-none shadow-sm hover:border-[#09090B] transition-colors
surfaces_and_micro_themes.estimate_grid: border-collapse border border-[#E4E4E7] bg-white rounded-none. Inner rows have border-b border-[#E4E4E7]. No rounded corners for a technical spreadsheet feel.
surfaces_and_micro_themes.sticky_totals_bar: bg-[#09090B] text-white backdrop-blur-xl bg-opacity-95 border-b sm:border-t-0 border-white/20 shadow-xl z-50
surfaces_and_micro_themes.primary_button: bg-[#F97316] text-[#09090B] font-bold rounded-none px-6 py-3 hover:bg-[#EA580C] active:translate-y-[1px] shadow-[4px_4px_0px_#09090B] border border-[#09090B] transition-all hover:shadow-[2px_2px_0px_#09090B] hover:translate-x-[2px] hover:translate-y-[2px]

components.inputs: bg-white border-2 border-[#E4E4E7] rounded-none focus:border-[#F97316] focus:ring-0 text-[#09090B] text-base md:text-sm font-['IBM_Plex_Sans']
components.number_inputs: Large tap targets, right-aligned text for easy numeric scanning. Spin buttons hidden.
components.tables: Flat, unstyled backgrounds. Header rows use overline typography. Zebra striping disabled in favor of strong 1px bottom borders.
components.badges: Square (rounded-none), uppercase, tiny text, bold border. e.g. 'border border-[#F97316] text-[#C2410C] px-2 py-1 text-xs font-bold' (darker orange for text — #F97316 fails 4.5:1 on white)

icons_strategy.style: Consistent stroke icons, w-4/w-5 sizing. NEVER use emoji glyphs as UI icons.

login_hero_bg.description: Architectural concrete and glass abstract - used for the split-screen login page background.

quote_watermark.description: Premium minimal textured background for the customer-facing PDF/Quote modal.

placeholder_job_photo.description: Vinyl siding house exterior to be used as a sample photo upload in the estimate.

user_avatar_placeholder.description: Modern construction foreman portrait for the top nav user avatar.

login.layout: CSS Grid: 1 col mobile, 2 cols desktop (md:grid-cols-2).
login.left_pane: White background, perfectly centered login form. Very sparse. Logo/Name at top, Email/Pass inputs, massive Orange primary button.
login.right_pane: Hidden on mobile. Uses `login_hero_bg` with `object-cover`. No overlay needed, let the image shine.

dashboard.layout: Standard web app layout. Top Navbar (white, 1px bottom border). Main content area with a max-width container.
dashboard.header: Title 'Estimates' left aligned (Cabinet Grotesk). Orange '+ New Estimate' button right aligned.
dashboard.list_view: Cardless list. Each estimate is a row with `border-b border-[#E4E4E7]`. Flexbox on mobile (stack details), Grid on desktop. Click anywhere on row to open.

estimate_editor.layout: The most complex screen. Needs to prioritize data density and mobile ergonomics.
estimate_editor.sticky_bar: Fixed to TOP of viewport. Black background (`bg-[#09090B]`). Displays: Job Name (left), Base Total, Sell Price (huge text, green/orange), Margin % (right). MUST always be visible.
estimate_editor.job_info_bento: Grid at the top (under sticky nav). Customer details, address, date. One cell dedicated to a dashed-border Photo Upload zone.
estimate_editor.margin_panel: A dedicated high-contrast block. Inputs for Sales Tax (%) and Target Profit Margin (%). Changing these dynamically recalculates the Sell Price in the sticky bar.
estimate_editor.categories_list: Render the 60+ items in collapsible Accordions (Shadcn) per category (Install Vinyl Siding, Ascend Accessories, etc.) to save scrolling.
estimate_editor.line_item_row: Inside category accordions. 3-column grid. Col 1: Item Name & Unit (text-sm). Col 2: Qty Input (large, h-12 to h-14, right-aligned text). Col 3: Line Total (read-only, calculated). Uses the Grid Borders technical look.

catalog_manager.content: List of all items. Inputs to edit Base Material Cost and Base Labor Cost directly in the row. 'Save Catalog' sticky button at bottom.

customer_quote_modal.layout: Modal or entirely separate print-only route. Must look like an expensive invoice.
customer_quote_modal.styling: Swiss Brutalist. Black text on white. NO orange buttons visible in the printable area. Crisp borders. Includes uploaded job photo.
customer_quote_modal.actions: Overlay action bar floating outside the printable area: 'Download PDF', 'Email to Customer', 'Close'.

testing_attributes: All inputs, buttons, and row items MUST use `data-testid` in kebab-case based on role. (e.g., `data-testid='login-submit-btn'`, `data-testid='qty-input-vinyl-siding'`).

contrast_rules: Text on light surfaces: >= 4.5:1. Muted text is #71717A (never #A1A1AA on white - 2.56:1 fail).
Orange as TEXT/ICON on light surfaces uses #C2410C (5.18:1); #F97316 is reserved for fills, borders, and text on dark surfaces (7.10:1 on #09090B).
Orange buttons use BLACK labels (#09090B on #F97316 = 7.10:1); white-on-orange (2.80:1) is forbidden.
On dark surfaces (#09090B), muted text stays #A1A1AA (7.76:1).
Focus indicators >= 3:1 against adjacent colors: use #C2410C border on white inputs.
accessibility.non_color_cues: Never rely on color alone: 'commonly needed' rows pair the yellow tint with a Lightbulb icon + count badge.

theming.system: Semantic CSS-variable tokens in frontend/src/index.css; themes are [data-theme=…] blocks. The colors in this document are the 'Safety Orange' default theme.
theming.validator: frontend/scripts/validate-themes.py — run after ANY token/theme change; every theme must pass all WCAG gates.
theming.rule: NEVER add new hardcoded hex Tailwind classes to themed UI — use the semantic tokens. Customer-facing documents (QuoteModal, AcceptPage, email/print HTML) stay unthemed.
